raw:
BORRK - Plan Additions

- The point here is to be a banking app for smart people.
- I want full stripe integration
- I want interest reports and projections on credit lines
- Configurable graphs and charts
- stock portfolio tracking
- Crypto tracking and wallet management
- wallet management
- Privacy.com integrations to create and track temporary limited cards
- Automated insurance appraisal. I want to integrate an SMTP server that can send emails to insurance brokers inquiring about prices. Then theyll report the reponses, and Ill get the best price.
- The same should go for all bills like phone bill, internet, gas, electricity, entertainment, rent, whatever.
- proactive problem solving. Can be configured to use an llm to analyze the situation and reccomend a pathway forward
optimized:
### OPT-001: Core Banking Platform

Build a personal finance banking app targeting power users. Rust backend, web frontend. Core features: account overview, transaction history, balance tracking, multi-currency support. Design for extensibility — all features below plug into this shell.

Source: Item 1

### OPT-002: Stripe Integration

Integrate Stripe API for payment processing, account funding, transfers, and card management. Support connected accounts, webhook handling, and transaction reconciliation with the core ledger.

Source: Item 2

### OPT-003: Credit Line Analytics

Build interest calculation engine for credit lines. Features: current interest accrual display, amortization schedules, payoff projections (minimum vs accelerated), and what-if scenarios for balance changes. Render as tabular reports and exportable summaries.

Source: Item 3

### OPT-004: Configurable Data Visualization

Implement a chart/graph system with user-configurable parameters. Support: line, bar, pie, area charts. Users select data source (spending, income, portfolio, credit), time range, grouping, and comparison overlays. Persist chart configurations per user.

Source: Item 4

### OPT-005: Stock Portfolio Tracker

Integrate market data API (e.g. Polygon.io, Alpha Vantage) for real-time and historical stock tracking. Features: holdings list with cost basis, gain/loss calculations, dividend tracking, sector allocation breakdown. Feed into the configurable chart system (OPT-004).

Source: Item 5

### OPT-006: Crypto Tracking and Wallet Management

Integrate crypto market data and wallet connectivity. Track holdings across wallets (hot/cold/exchange) with unified portfolio view. Support fiat wallet balances and multi-currency management. Features: real-time prices, transaction history per wallet, aggregate P&L, gas fee tracking. Merge fiat and crypto wallet views into a single wallet management interface.

Source: Items 6 and 7 (merged)

### OPT-007: Privacy.com Virtual Card Integration

Integrate Privacy.com API to create, manage, and track virtual cards. Features: create single-use and merchant-locked cards, set spending limits, pause/close cards, transaction feed per card. Link virtual card spend back to the core ledger for categorization.

Source: Item 8

### OPT-008: Automated Bill Negotiation System

Build an automated competitive-pricing engine for recurring expenses. Architecture:

1. **SMTP integration** — send templated inquiry emails to providers/brokers requesting quotes.
2. **Response parser** — ingest reply emails, extract quoted prices using rule-based parsing + optional LLM fallback.
3. **Comparison engine** — rank responses by price, terms, and coverage. Present best options to user with recommendation.
4. **Bill categories** — insurance (home, auto, health, life), phone, internet, gas, electricity, entertainment subscriptions, rent, and user-defined categories.
5. **Scheduling** — configurable cadence per bill category (e.g. annually for insurance, quarterly for utilities).

User reviews and approves all outbound communications before sending.

Source: Items 9 and 10 (merged)

### OPT-009: LLM-Powered Financial Advisory

Integrate an LLM analysis layer (configurable provider: local Ollama, Claude API, OpenAI). Features: analyze spending patterns, flag anomalies, recommend actions (e.g. "refinance at current rates saves $X/mo"), surface upcoming bill increases, and suggest negotiation triggers for OPT-008. User enables/disables per category. All recommendations are advisory — no automated actions without explicit approval.

Source: Item 11
